WARNING: These test points are for A2918 (820-02757) ONLY. For other boards, use that board's diagnostic files. Component designators and test points are board-specific — the same rail appears at DIFFERENT components on other boards.
description: Decision trees for all 5V DCPS scenarios. Board connected via USB-C at 5V (pre-PD negotiation). Current draw indicates where in the power chain the fault lies. Note: A2918 has different test points than A2681 — use TPU633/TPU630/TPU636 from FCT pages.

scenarios.meaning: Board draws nothing. CD3217 is not even loading the VBUS line. Fault is at the input stage — connector, cable, or CD3217 itself.

steps.measure: Visual inspection of connector pins (MJ722 connector, page 66)
steps.expected: Clean pins, no bent/missing contacts, no liquid residue
steps.if_wrong: Replace USB-C connector flex or clean connector

steps.measure: Probe CF401 (PPVBUS_USBC0 decoupling)
steps.if_wrong: DCPS cable or connector open circuit. Try other port (CF503 for ATC1). If both dead, connector flex fault.

steps.action: Check CD3217 (UF400) for signs of life
steps.measure: Diode mode on UF400 VDD pins
steps.if_wrong: UF400 short to ground → replace CD3217. Check SPI ROM for short.

steps.measure: Move DCPS to second port, probe CF503
steps.if_wrong: Both ports dead → likely upstream issue (connector flex, liquid damage to both CD3217s)
steps.if_ok: First port CD3217 (UF400) fault. Second port works → replace UF400.

scenarios.meaning: CD3217 is drawing some current but board is not progressing through boot. Most common 5V scenario. The 5V is reaching the charger circuit but PPBUS_AON is likely not coming up, or PP3V8_AON is not.

steps.measure: Probe TPU633 (PPBUS_AON test point from FCT page 113)
steps.expected: 12V (battery voltage) or rising toward ~5V (adapter only, pre-PD)
steps.if_missing: Fault in charging circuit. Go to step 2.

steps.measure: Diode mode on PPBUS_AON bulk caps
steps.if_wrong: PPBUS_AON shorted. Thermal cam to find hot component.
steps.next_if_ok: Check charger IC (U5200 area) and CD3217 communication.

steps.measure: Probe TPU630 (PP3V8_AON test point from FCT page 113)
steps.if_missing: Fault in U5700 (SVR AON). Check PPVAON_PWR_EN (delayed enable via D5340/R5340/R5341 on page 30). Check for short on PP3V8_AON line.

steps.measure: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON distribution: VDDMAIN, WLBT_1SNS, HDMI_1SNS, SPKAMP_LEFT/RIGHT_1SNS
steps.if_wrong: PP3V8_AON branch shorted. Thermal cam to find hot component. Common: WiFi/BT module short, HDMI circuit short (Pro-specific).

steps.measure: Probe TPU636 (PP5V_S2 test point from FCT page 113)
steps.if_missing: UC260 (TPS62130) fault or PPV5S2TS_PWR_EN not asserted.

steps.measure: Probe MPMU analog probe points (page 47, PP57xx series)
steps.if_all_present: Fault is downstream of PMU — SoC domain or peripheral. Thermal cam.

steps.measure: Apply thermal cam to the rail that is missing/shorted

scenarios.meaning: Board is drawing moderate current at 5V. Charger circuit is partially working. PP3V8_AON likely present but something downstream is loading it.

steps.measure: Probe TPU633
steps.if_missing: Charger path fault. See 0.001-0.05A tree step 2.

steps.measure: Probe TPU630

steps.action: Check PP3V8_AON branches — HDMI and SD card (Pro-specific)
steps.measure: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON_HDMI_1SNS and PP3V3_MAME_S60 paths
steps.if_wrong: HDMI or SD card circuit pulling PP3V8_AON down. Isolate by removing suspect loads.
steps.note: Pro-specific: A2918 has HDMI Cobra (UH700) and SD card (UK550) drawing from PP3V8_AON that Air models don't have.

steps.measure: Probe TPU636
steps.if_missing: Check UC260 (TPS62130) and its input/output passives.
steps.if_present: Run full MPMU LDO check per step 6 in 0.001-0.05A tree.

scenarios.range: 0.2-0.5A (significant current, boot stalled or shorted rail)
scenarios.meaning: Board is drawing significant current at 5V. Either trying to boot but stalling, or a rail has a short pulling current.

steps.action: Is the board trying to boot? Check for signs of life.
steps.measure: Look for fan spin, keyboard backlight, display backlight flash, chime
steps.if_signs: Board is booting at 5V but not completing PD negotiation. Check CD3217 firmware ROM. Go to step 5.
steps.if_no_signs: Board is drawing current into a short or stalled PMU. Go to step 2.

steps.measure: TPU633→TPU630→TPU636→MPMU LDOs
steps.if_any_missing: Fault identified at the missing rail.

steps.action: Check PP3V3_S2_LDO and PP3V3_UPC lines for shorts
steps.measure: Diode mode on PP3V3_S2_LDO, PP3V3_UPC0_LDO, PP3V3_UPC1_LDO
steps.if_wrong: PP3V3 rail shorted. CD3217 may be causing the short.

steps.measure: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON distribution caps
steps.if_wrong: PP3V8_AON short. Thermal cam to isolate.

steps.action: Connect to 20V DCPS and check if board transitions
steps.measure: Switch DCPS to 20V, observe current
steps.if_normal_boot: Board boots at 20V. Problem was CD3217 PD negotiation. Replace SPI ROM or CD3217.
steps.if_same_current: Short is genuine. Thermal cam all power domains.

scenarios.range: 0.5A+ (high current at 5V — major short or boot attempt)
scenarios.meaning: Very high current at 5V. Either a serious short on a high-current rail or the board is attempting to boot.

steps.measure: Remove battery flex (J5151)
steps.note: High current at 5V with battery can cause heating.

steps.measure: Diode mode on PPBUS_AON bulk caps
steps.if_wrong: PPBUS_AON dead short. Thermal cam immediately.

steps.measure: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON and VDDMAIN distribution
steps.if_wrong: PP3V8_AON dead short. U5700 pulling max current into short. Thermal cam.

steps.action: Check if board is actually booting
steps.measure: Switch to 20V DCPS
steps.expected: If board boots normally at 20V with 1.5-3A, the 5V high current was a boot attempt.
steps.if_boots: Normal. Board was trying to boot at 5V. No fault.
steps.if_same_high_current: Genuine short. Systematic thermal cam sweep.
